title: Disposable BBQ was likely cause of fire in Macclesfield forest

input.lead: Fire crews worked through the night to tackle the wildfire in Macclesfield, Cheshire
input.summary: Fire crews worked through the night to tackle the wildfire in Macclesfield, Cheshire
input.evidence_text: A large woodland and grass fire tackled overnight was probably caused by a disposable barbecue, the fire service has said Dozens of firefighters were called to the wildfire at Hacked Way Lane, near Macclesfield, Cheshire, just after 16:00 BST on Tuesday Ten fire engines and specialist units were there at its height with United Utilities helping with water drops by helicopter Cheshire Fire and Rescue service said a discarded barbecue was the "probable cause" and crews were still at the scene on Wednesday morning It was a reminder of the "devastating impact that careless behaviour can have on our countryside, particularly during periods of warm, dry weather", they said The fire service urged people to stay away from the area , saying visitors continued to arrive while emergency crews worked nearby

final_reason: Cheap pre-enrich duplicate — same URL/title kept from stronger source.

title: Did Andy Burnham really fail to tackle homelessness as mayor?

input.lead: Critics say he failed to meet his 2020 pledge, but others say he deserves credit for his leadership
input.summary: Critics say he failed to meet his 2020 pledge, but others say he deserves credit for his leadership
input.evidence_text: Andy Burnham has twice pledged to end rough sleeping - when he was elected mayor of Greater Manchester and again in front of 10 Downing Street on Monday, in his first speech as prime minister His promise as mayor to end rough sleeping by 2020 appeared successful at first, with figures falling from 268 people sleeping on the street in 2017, to 90 in 2021 But the dip also coincided with a national effort from councils to bring rough sleepers inside during the Covid-19 pandemic, and the figure has begun to rise again alongside hikes in the cost of living Some have criticised Burnham's failure to keep his promise as mayor, while others say 'the story in Manchester is positive' There were high-profile flashpoints in the city during his time as mayor, including the clearance of tents around St Peter's Square Last year, Manchester City Council said refugees were particularly drawn to Manchester as a "friendly global city", but many were falling into rough sleeping as support services grew overloaded with rising demand

title: Olympic medal and large bear figure among unusual police seizures

input.lead: Alongside gold and designer watches, police say criminals have been making some unusual investments
input.summary: Alongside gold and designer watches, police say criminals have been making some unusual investments
input.evidence_text: Boxing belts, an Olympic medal and a giant bear figure are all amongst items that have been confiscated from criminals in Greater Manchester A record amount of money, totalling £18.2m, was recovered from criminals across Greater Manchester from April 2025 to April 2026, according to police As well as the cash, police also seized items, from designer watches and gold to more unusual items among the haul BBC North West Tonight visited the Greater Manchester Police's evidence store to take a look at the wide-ranging collection of confiscated goods, from sports memorabilia to rare collectible toys Staff showed the BBC a signed and framed Floyd Mayweather boxing belt, a signed and framed football shirt from former Manchester City star João Cancelo and an Olympic gold medal and cap, also signed and mounted "Money launderers have got to try and find way to disguise the proceeds of their crime, so they will invest in some quite unusual items", Det Supt Andrew Buckthorpe said

final_reason: Cheap pre-enrich duplicate — same URL/title kept from stronger source.

title: 'Lip King' banned from carrying out BBLs in two UK areas before woman's death

input.lead: Alice Webb's family say she would have never had a BBL if she knew about previous bans
input.summary: Alice Webb's family say she would have never had a BBL if she knew about previous bans
input.evidence_text: A cosmetic practitioner arrested after the death of a mother-of-five had been banned from carrying out liquid Brazilian Butt Lift (BBL) procedures in two other parts of England, the BBC has learned Jordan Parke, known as the "Lip King", faced enforcement action in Dudley and Greater Manchester before treating Alice Webb in Gloucester in 2024 Webb's former partner, Ben Kingscote, said the case highlighted gaps in regulation and called for a national system to prevent practitioners operating elsewhere A Department of Health and Social Care spokesperson said ministers would consult on tougher measures covering high-risk cosmetic procedures, including liquid BBLs A BBL is a non-surgical cosmetic procedure in which dermal fillers are injected into the buttocks to alter their size or shape 'I was abused after meeting man on dating app'

final_reason: Cheap pre-enrich duplicate — same URL/title kept from stronger source.

title: Son who killed his mum in Macclesfield home given hospital order

input.lead: Psychiatric evidence indicated Simon Carter had paranoid schizophrenia, prosecutors say
input.summary: Psychiatric evidence indicated Simon Carter had paranoid schizophrenia, prosecutors say
input.evidence_text: A man who killed his "hardworking" mother at her Macclesfield home has been given a hospital order with no fixed end date Simon Norman Carter, 50, of no fixed address, pleaded guilty to the manslaughter of Gwyneth Carter, 72, on the grounds of diminished responsibility Her family remembered her as strong and protective, with a laugh people would not forget The Crown Prosecution Service said psychiatric evidence indicated Carter was suffering from paranoid schizophrenia At Chester Crown Court on 20 July, Judge Everett imposed a hospital order under section 37 of the Mental Health Act 1983, with a restriction order under section 41 Cheshire Police said Carter, who had been living in Torquay, had been estranged from his mother for 19 years when he arrived unannounced at her Pearson Street home on 12 July 2025

final_reason: Intra-batch topic duplicate — same story kept from stronger source.

title: Boy, 15, detained after man found dead in Bolton

input.lead: A 15-year-old boy has been detained under the Mental Health Act as police investigate
input.summary: A 15-year-old boy has been detained under the Mental Health Act as police investigate
input.evidence_text: Boy, 15, detained after man found dead in Bolton - BBC News Image source, PA Image caption, A murder investigation has been launched after a man was found dead in Bolton on 30 June By Georgie Docker North West Published 21 July 2026 A 15-year-old boy has been detained after a man was found dead at a home in Bolton. A murder investigation has been launched after 50-year-old man was discovered at a house on Gray Street, on 30 June. A 15-year-old boy has been detained under the Mental Health Act and will be questioned, police said. Police believe it to be an isolated incident - adding there is "no wider risk to the local community". "Our thoughts remain firmly with the man's family and friends," Detective Chief Inspector Louise Edwards said. "Following new information coming to light, we are now carrying out enquiries to establish the full circumstances surrounding the events of the day he died." She added: "There is no information to suggest there is any wider risk to the public, and we are not seeking anyone else in relation to this incident." title: Mum's 'tears of joy' as her daughter finally leaves hospital unit

input.lead: Natasha spent more than 14 months in hospital due to uncertainty about who should fund her complex care
input.summary: Natasha spent more than 14 months in hospital due to uncertainty about who should fund her complex care
input.evidence_text: A woman who was stuck in hospital for months while the NHS worked out who should fund her complex care package has been allowed to leave, just days after her plight was highlighted by the BBC Natasha has a rare progressive neurological disease and requires one-to-one care jointly funded by her council and the local NHS Her mum, Elaine, had long battled to secure a care package for the 27-year-old from Southport and while one had been ready since May, Natasha was unable to move until a funding deal was reached "She's going to have an amazing life," said Elaine of Natasha's move. "I'm just so excited for her. I never believed this day was going to come." Natasha was born with a rare degenerative disorder called Leigh's Disease , external , which affects the brain and spinal cord She also has GAMT deficiency, which affects brain development and causes epileptic seizures

title: Why voting method change matters in mayoral poll

input.lead: Why the Supplementary Vote may allow voters to vote with their heads and their hearts
input.summary: Why the Supplementary Vote may allow voters to vote with their heads and their hearts
input.evidence_text: The Labour Party could benefit most from the return of the Supplementary Votes (SV) system in the Greater Manchester mayoral race, a polling expert has said Voters in the region head to the polls on 30 July and will be able to select a first and second choice of candidate as SV comes into replace the first-past-the-post (FPTP) system used at the last election A number of candidates have already declared for the contest , triggered after Andy Burnham's successful campaign to become the MP of Makerfield, leaving the post he has held since 2017 vacant Prof Jonathan Tonge of the University of Liverpool said Labour could pick up second preference votes from Greens and Lib Dems, while Reform could be less likely to see Restore voters put them as second preference due to "bad blood" between the party leaders The system was used in the first two mayoral elections, but was scrapped by the last Conservative government in favour of FPTP system, before it was reintroduced again by Labour Under SCV, candidates need to win more than 50% of the vote in the first round to be come mayor or the top two candidates go into a run-off where second preference votes from eliminated candidates are counted

final_reason: Dedupe: live item has no exact/history repeat; accepted for editorial selection. | Validator: news item is 18 days old (hard cutoff 14d), dropped regardless of update wording.

title: What is the Greater Manchester mayor and what do they do?

input.lead: Greater Manchester goes to the polls on 30 July, but what powers does the region's mayor hold?
input.summary: Greater Manchester goes to the polls on 30 July, but what powers does the region's mayor hold?
input.evidence_text: Andy Burnham's victory in the Makerfield by-election means he has to step down as Greater Manchester mayor Burnham has been mayor since the role was first created in 2017 and the race to become his successor will be fiercely contested by all involved But what is the role they will be fighting for? The role of mayor was created as part of a law passed in 2016, one of a number of big pieces of devolution legislation brought in by the Conservatives after taking power in 2010 In a 2012 referendum, Manchester rejected having a directly-elected city mayor, along with eight other major cities, but the decision to have a regional or metropolitan mayor was directly negotiated between the government and the 10 Greater Manchester councils Andy Burnham, who left Parliament to stand for the role, was chosen as Labour's candidate for the first mayoral election in 2017 and won, taking 63.4% of the vote

title: Greater Manchester's mayoral election explained

input.lead: Voters in all 10 boroughs will elect a new mayor to replace Andy Burnham on 30 July
input.summary: Voters in all 10 boroughs will elect a new mayor to replace Andy Burnham on 30 July
input.evidence_text: People living in Greater Manchester will head to the polls in July to vote for who they think should be the region's mayor Residents living in all 10 boroughs will vote in the Greater Manchester mayoral by-election on 30 July We take a look at why it is happening, what the timetable is, and how the supplementary vote system works The election was triggered when Andy Burnham, who held the office for nine years, was elected the MP for Makerfield in last Thursday's parliamentary by-election By law, an MP cannot sit in the House of Commons and hold a metropolitan mayor role that has powers over local policing The Greater Manchester mayoral contest has to take place within 35 days of the post becoming vacant

title: People lighting BBQs near Dove Stone and Swineshaw moorland fires

input.lead: Fire crews say they have had to attend to fires started by campers while tackling moorland blazes
input.summary: Fire crews say they have had to attend to fires started by campers while tackling moorland blazes
input.evidence_text: Visitors have been lighting BBQs near the sites of major moorland fires, firefighters in Greater Manchester have said As work continues in Dove Stone and Swineshaw as part of the ongoing response to the fires, firefighters have attended a small fire on the moors linked to camping Preventable fires risk diverting firefighters, vehicles and equipment away from the ongoing response, Greater Manchester Fire and Rescue Service (GMFRS) said The warning comes as the service is preparing to reduce the number of crews it has at the site overnight A GMFRS spokesperson said the national teams that had been on hand to support the service had "now been released", though some specialists remained to support the continued effort "Current plans are to maintain resources through the week and into the weekend, with ongoing dry weather continuing to present operational challenges," they added

title: Warrington Council's sale of £30m Redwood Bank shares collapses

input.lead: The council says a further offer has been received, but there will be "new and significant costs"
input.summary: The council says a further offer has been received, but there will be "new and significant costs"
input.evidence_text: The planned sale of a council's £30m stake in a bank has fallen though, the authority confirmed Warrington Council bought a third share of Redwood Bank in 2017 and said earlier this year it had agreed to sell its share at a "substantial financial loss" . It has now said this sale has collapsed, because of "macroeconomic conditions" Warrington Council built up £1.8bn of debt linked to its investment strategy in recent years, which led to the government appointing ministerial envoys to work with the authority last year The authority said it has received a further offer for its shares in the bank, from a third party not linked to the original bid In a report to the council's cabinet ahead of a meeting on Monday (27 July), it said the offer would be "confirmed through due diligence" but no more details have been provided The report said the council had already incurred "significant costs" on matters relating to the sale, and that there would be "new and significant costs" linked to a new offer

title: Woman's life as a refugee inspired her public health degree

input.lead: Makagbeh Princess Sesay has earned a degree in public health from the University of Manchester
input.summary: Makagbeh Princess Sesay has earned a degree in public health from the University of Manchester
input.evidence_text: A woman who fled civil war as a child has said she wants to use her degree to help "other refugees and vulnerable communities" Makagbeh Princess Sesay left Liberia in 1990 and spent 10 years in refugee camps volunteering with humanitarian organisations before resettling in Europe in 2003 The now 52-year-old said graduating with a BSc in public health from the University of Manchester on Monday had been a "dream" come true She said growing up as a refugee showed her the importance of "healthcare, education, sanitation and nutrition" and her studies had strengthened her "desire to support vulnerable communities and work towards reducing health inequalities" Sesay crossed into neighbouring Guinea from Liberia in a truck with her family as they fled the conflict in December 1990 Her father died in Liberia shortly after the conflict began and her mother later died in the Guinean border town of Koyama

title: Leaky dams on Holcombe Moor to stop flooding and wildfires

input.lead: A series of 'leaky dams' have been created on the moors surrounding Greater Manchester to stop wildfires from spreading and to prevent flooding in the valleys below The wooden dams have been installed in gullies by The National Trust and United Utilities across Holcombe Moor, par
input.summary: A series of 'leaky dams' have been created on the moors surrounding Greater Manchester to stop wildfires from spreading and to prevent flooding in the valleys below The wooden dams have been installed in gullies by The National Trust and United Utilities across Holcombe Moor, par
input.evidence_text: A series of 'leaky dams' have been created on the moors surrounding Greater Manchester to stop wildfires from spreading and to prevent flooding in the valleys below The wooden dams have been installed in gullies by The National Trust and United Utilities across Holcombe Moor, part of the Stubbins Estate above Ramsbottom Nik Taylor, 57, a National Trust Ranger for Holcombe Moor and the Stubbins Estate, said: "Keeping the moors wet by raising ground water levels is really important when we're in a period of hot weather because it can help create fire-breaks." United Utilities said using the peat land as "a natural sponge" can keep water on the moors giving multiple benefits Taylor said: "We use leaky dams to control the flow of water into the River Irwell and it's an essential part of the restoration of our peat lands More than 1,000 acres of land at Holcombe Moor was transferred from the Ministry of Defence to the National Trust in 1994

Sitting in 11F, objects flew through the air and out the window as other passengers desperately tried to hold him in place The passenger was left with burns and bruises on his right arm, which stretch from his his armpit to his chest. Now, with his head supported by a neck brace, Ljubisa has shared his account of what happened on the flight "The explosion is what I remember," Ljubisa, an entrepreneur, told The Guardian . "Its the noise before the chaos, the noise that is always there when I close my eyes to sleep." He was partially sucked out of the window before being pulled back in. Rob Williams reports. View 5 Images Andy Burnham arrived to a round of applau News Rob Williams Features and Perspectives Editor 18:59, 21 Jul 2026 “Can I just say, I expect this at every meeting”. Andy Burnham’s first Cabinet meeting as Prime Minister began with a rapturous round of applause. If he felt comfortable in the room it was partly that many of t Mr Burnham has constructed the most northern cabinet in modern British political history. Of the 23 full members, including the Prime Minister, 11 represent constituencies in northern England. When the wider group of ministers eligible to attend Cabinet is included, that rises to That is a significant change from the previous Labour administration. Under Sir Keir Starmer’s final Cabinet, 10 of 25 Cabinet attendees represented northern constituencies View 5 Images Burnham's new Cabinet at their first meeting in Downing Street (Image: Eddie Mulholland/Daily Telegraph/PA Wire) The increase is particularly pronounced in the North West. The number of Cabinet attendees representing North West seats has risen from two under Starmer, Lisa Nandy and Jonathan Reynolds, to seven under Burnham. They are the Prime Minister himself, Manchester Central MP Lucy Pow

input.evidence_text: Castleton Major 158-home affordable scheme in Rochdale given final sign-off The housing scheme was approved last year by the council's planning committee, but now work can actually start Comments News George Lythgoe Local Democracy Reporter 18:03, 21 A 158-home development in Castleton has been given the final sign-off by Rochdale council The scheme will see the mix of two and three-bedroom houses built on Trub Farm off Manchester Road. MCI Developments will take the long stretch of land bounded by the canal, just a short distance from Castleton town centre, and transform it into a residential space Private gardens, a children’s play park and public open space have been factored into the plans All the homes will be available at an affordable rate (up to 80 per cent of market value). Most of the accommodation will be provided as semi-detached houses or small terraces of three. In total, 48 of the homes will be apartments spread across two, three storey tower blocks Access to the site will come from Trub Road, via a bridge and walkway over the canal to allow pedestrians to join the existing connection onto the canal footpath. There is also a proposed stairway to the north of the site, leading up to the canal, which will allow easy connection

This would be for both St Phillip’s Catholic Primary and Dial Park Primary on the road A School Streets scheme is a national programme across the country to introduce temporary vehicle restrictions on roads near a school to create a safer environment at drop off and pick up times They can be enforced through a number of different means including numberplate recognition cameras and can involve the entire road being shut off After the schools wanted to be included in the scheme, funding was awarded by Transport for Greater Manchester to move ahead but the matter will need to be approached by Stockport Council’s area committee for Stepping Hill

Sitting in 11F, objects flew through the air and out the window as other passengers desperately tried to hold him in place The passenger was left with burns and bruises on his right arm, which stretch from his his armpit to his chest. Now, with his head supported by a neck brace, Ljubisa has shared his account of what happened on the flight "The explosion is what I remember," Ljubisa, an entrepreneur, told The Guardian . "Its the noise before the chaos, the noise that is always there when I close my eyes to sleep." He was partially sucked out of the window before being pulled back in. Sitting in 11F, objects flew through the air and out the window as other passengers desperately tried to hold him in place The passenger was left with burns and bruises on his right arm, which stretch from his his armpit to his chest. Now, with his head supported by a neck brace, Ljubisa has shared his account of what happened on the flight "The explosion is what I remember," Ljubisa, an entrepreneur, told The Guardian . "Its the noise before the chaos, the noise that is always there when I close my eyes to sleep." He was partially sucked out of the window before being pulled back in. Ljubisa is understood to have fainted between two or three times during the ordeal, according to his lawyer

Andy Burnham’s first Cabinet meeting as Prime Minister began with a rapturous round of applause. If he felt comfortable in the room it was partly that many of t Mr Burnham has constructed the most northern cabinet in modern British political history. Of the 23 full members, including the Prime Minister, 11 represent constituencies in northern England. When the wider group of ministers eligible to attend Cabinet is included, that rises to That is a significant change from the previous Labour administration. Under Sir Keir Starmer’s final Cabinet, 10 of 25 Cabinet attendees represented northern constituencies View 5 Images Burnham's new Cabinet at their first meeting in Downing Street (Image: Eddie Mulholland/Daily Telegraph/PA Wire) The increase is particularly pronounced in the North West. The number of Cabinet attendees representing North West seats has risen from two under Starmer, Lisa Nandy and Jonathan Reynolds, to seven under Burnham. input.evidence_text: Monday 13 July 2026 at 3:43pm Play Brightcove video Sarah and husband Martin has been speaking to ITV Granada Reports’ Emma Sweeney A breast cancer patient from Greater Manchester says she’s devastated and angry that a drug that she believes could extend her life is still not available on the NHS in England Sarah Barchetti, who has been living with secondary breast cancer for seven years, has been treated at The Christie hospital in Manchester throughout her illness. title: Andy Burnham wins Makerfield by-election: What happens next with Greater Manchester mayoralty?

input.summary: Friday 19 June 2026 at 1:43pm Andy Burnham has been mayor of Greater Manchester for almost a decade. Credit: PA Images Andy Burnham’s historic victory in the Makerfield by-election sets off a rapid chain reaction for both Greater Manchester and the wider UK political landscape A
input.evidence_text: Friday 19 June 2026 at 1:43pm Andy Burnham has been mayor of Greater Manchester for almost a decade. Credit: PA Images Andy Burnham’s historic victory in the Makerfield by-election sets off a rapid chain reaction for both Greater Manchester and the wider UK political landscape A metro mayor cannot simultaneously serve as an MP, a clear legal and electoral process is now underway to find someone new to take on the role Andy Burnham has won a seat as an MP - but can he still be the Mayor of Greater Manchester? No. Andy Burnham cannot be mayor and a member of parliament. He had seven days - from the moment of his election - to resign from the post which he has held for almost a decade He handed in his resignation on 19 June - triggering the process of the by-election Burnham cannot do both jobs because the Mayor of Greater Manchester holds Police and Crime Commissioner (PCC) powers and UK law strictly dictates that the office holder cannot simultaneously serve as an MP

input.evidence_text: A record amount of money, totalling £18.2m, was recovered from criminals across Greater Manchester from April 2025 to April 2026, according to police As well as the cash, police also seized items, from designer watches and gold to more unusual items among the haul BBC North West Tonight visited the Greater Manchester Police's evidence store to take a look at the wide-ranging collection of confiscated goods, from sports memorabilia to rare collectible toys Staff showed the BBC a signed and framed Floyd Mayweather boxing belt, a signed and framed football shirt from former Manchester City star João Cancelo and an Olympic gold medal and cap, also signed and mounted "Money launderers have got to try and find way to disguise the proceeds of their crime, so they will invest in some quite unusual items", Det Supt Andrew Buckthorpe said Money from the confiscated assets and forfeited cash will go into the government's Asset Recovery Incentivisation Scheme, where 50% is kept by the government but the rest is distributed back to the police force area where the crime took place

title: 'Lip King' banned from carrying out BBLs in two UK areas before woman's death

input.lead: Jordan Parke, known as the "Lip King", faced enforcement action in Dudley and Greater Manchester before treating Alice Webb in Gloucester in 2024 Webb's former partner, Ben Kingscote, said the case highlighted gaps in regulation and called for a national system to prevent practit
input.summary: Jordan Parke, known as the "Lip King", faced enforcement action in Dudley and Greater Manchester before treating Alice Webb in Gloucester in 2024 Webb's former partner, Ben Kingscote, said the case highlighted gaps in regulation and called for a national system to prevent practit
input.evidence_text: Jordan Parke, known as the "Lip King", faced enforcement action in Dudley and Greater Manchester before treating Alice Webb in Gloucester in 2024 Webb's former partner, Ben Kingscote, said the case highlighted gaps in regulation and called for a national system to prevent practitioners operating elsewhere A Department of Health and Social Care spokesperson said ministers would consult on tougher measures covering high-risk cosmetic procedures, including liquid BBLs A BBL is a non-surgical cosmetic procedure in which dermal fillers are injected into the buttocks to alter their size or shape Kingscote said Webb, from Wotton-under-Edge in Gloucestershire, would not have undergone the procedure had she known about the previous enforcement action "If Alice had known about these prohibition orders she wouldn't have had it done ," he said

input.evidence_text: Wildfires are more common in the UK than many people realise, although most are typically small and last a short time. Climate change projections suggest we will see conditions mo In 2025, fire and rescue services in England and Wales responded to nearly 1,000 wildfire incidents - surpassing the previous record set in 2022. This year, widespread wildfires have led to headlines about a ‘firewave’. With hotter, drier summers becoming more common, it’s worth A wildfire is any uncontrolled vegetation fire serious enough to require an emergency response. Typically, they include fires covering at least a hectare of land (approximately the size of 2 rugby pitches), with flames over 1.5 metres high, or incidents requiring fire crews for 6 Increasingly, yes. Climate change is making our summers hotter and drier - ideal conditions for fire. When landscapes experience several consecutive days of dry weather and little or no rain, plants and soils lose moisture faster than they can recover. Vegetation becomes a more a It takes very little vegetation (fuel) for a wildfire to start and it is important to follow wildfire prevention advice issued by the Fire and Rescue Service . Most wildfires start due to human activities and are a risk to communities, emergency services as well as increasing pre All smoke is potentially harmful to your health. title: Smoke from wildfire affecting air quality in some areas

input.lead: Greater Manchester Fire and Rescue Service (GMFRS) and partner agencies continue to respond to the moorland fire near Dovestone Reservoir
input.summary: Greater Manchester Fire and Rescue Service (GMFRS) and partner agencies continue to respond to the moorland fire near Dovestone Reservoir
input.evidence_text: Greater Manchester Fire and Rescue Service (GMFRS) and partner agencies continue to respond to the moorland fire near Dovestone Reservoir Strong winds have carried smoke across parts of Greater Manchester, including Manchester Residents should avoid areas affected by smoke wherever possible. If smoke is visible in your area, stay indoors with doors and windows closed. This is the most effective way to reduce exposure to wildfire smoke Wildfire smoke can irritate the eyes, nose, throat and lungs. Most healthy people will only experience temporary symptoms, but smoke can have a greater impact on babies and young children, older people, pregnant people, and those with existing heart or lung conditions For most people any symptoms will be temporary and will quickly disappear once you are away from the smoke. However, if symptoms persist or get worse, you should seek health advice Symptoms may include irritated eyes and airways, coughing, wheezing, shortness of breath and chest discomfort. title: Oldham bucks national trend as more young people choose education and training

input.lead: Oldham has recorded participation rates above both regional and national averages while significantly reducing the number of young people who are not in education, employment or training (NEET)
input.summary: Oldham has recorded participation rates above both regional and national averages while significantly reducing the number of young people who are not in education, employment or training (NEET)
input.evidence_text: Oldham has recorded participation rates above both regional and national averages while significantly reducing the number of young people who are not in education, employment or training (NEET) The latest Department for Education (DfE) Local Authority Participation and NEET figures for the 2025/26 academic year show that 93.2 per cent of Oldham's 16 and 17-year-olds are participating in education and training, an increase of 2.8 percentage points on the previous year. T At the same time, Oldham's combined NEET and Not Known (NK) rate has fallen to 4.6%, down 1.1 percentage points from 2024/25. This is significantly lower than both the North West average of 5.5% and the England average of 5.8% The figures also highlight Oldham's strong performance, in partnership with the charity Positive Steps, in tracking and supporting young people Positive Steps provides young people with support, careers advice and guidance after school, or between 19 and 25 if they have an Education Healthcare Plan (EHCP), helping them to move into education, training or employment Only 59 young people out of 7,177 are currently unaccounted for – 0.8 per cent of the borough's young people. This is well below the North West average of 1.3 per cent and the England average of 2.4 per cent

The building also includes two flexible spaces for school visits, community events, meetings, exhibitions and performances When the site opens, visitors will be able to enjoy an expanding network of accessible green spaces, orchards, community gardens, food-growing areas and nature-rich landscapes. title: Encouraging decline in youth smoking and vaping across Wigan Borough

input.evidence_text: Wigan Borough is seeing a positive decline in the number of young people smoking and regularly vaping, according to a recent Trading Standards survey Following national concerns about youth smoking and vaping, Wigan Council has made it a priority to reduce these figures locally and protect the health of our children and young people With more than 1,000 school children aged 14-17 included in the Trading Standards survey, the findings show the number of young people who have tried vaping dropped from around 44 per cent in 2023 to 18 per cent in 2026 The survey, carried out between November 2025 and February 2026, also highlights that smoking is at an all-time low, with around 97 per cent reporting to have never tried smoking Councillor Keith Cunliffe, Deputy Leader of Wigan Council and cabinet portfolio holder for adult social care and health, said: “I’m really pleased to see smoking and vaping rates among young people in Wigan Borough continuing to fall “Reducing tobacco and nicotine use among young people is one of the most effective ways to prevent long-term ill health and reduce health inequalities in our communities

input.summary: Inhale Me, Exhale You By Kate Partington Tue 2 Jun 10:00 - Mon 28 Sep 22:00 Art Tue 2 Jun - Mon 28 Sep From 10:00 Tue 2 Jun - Mon 28 Sep From 10:00 Arches Windows No booking required Unspoken feelings and moments of desire You can see this artwork in HOME Arches Windows on Whitwo
input.evidence_text: Inhale Me, Exhale You By Kate Partington Tue 2 Jun 10:00 - Mon 28 Sep 22:00 Art Tue 2 Jun - Mon 28 Sep From 10:00 Tue 2 Jun - Mon 28 Sep From 10:00 Arches Windows No booking required Unspoken feelings and moments of desire You can see this artwork in HOME Arches Windows on Whitwo This piece belongs to a wider series called ‘Desire Me.’ It aims to translate unspoken feelings and moments of desire that occur between us The focus is on longing, on reaching out and on instances of hopeful touch that happen in the early stages of a relationship. It seeks to capture the essence of sensations you get in your stomach that are so hard to verbalise, but that we all know and feel.” Kate Partington’s practice explores the subtleties of touch, connection, and the spaces that exist between bodies She is a mixed media artist working across analogue and digital spaces, constructing images that hover between materiality and emotion Her work examines how intimacy can be both tender and elusive, how connection can be felt rather than seen

title: Marina Abramović: Balkan Erotic Epic | Aviva Studios

input.lead: Experience the raw, unfiltered power of Balkan folklore in a new immersive show from the world’s most famous performance artist Balkan Erotic Epic is a bold new performance work created by the iconic artist Marina Abramović .
input.summary: Experience the raw, unfiltered power of Balkan folklore in a new immersive show from the world’s most famous performance artist Balkan Erotic Epic is a bold new performance work created by the iconic artist Marina Abramović . Regarded as the world’s most influential performance a
input.evidence_text: Experience the raw, unfiltered power of Balkan folklore in a new immersive show from the world’s most famous performance artist Balkan Erotic Epic is a bold new performance work created by the iconic artist Marina Abramović . Regarded as the world’s most influential performance artist, Abramović’s transformative work has reached millions of people – from opening the Pyramid Stage at Glastonbury Festival t A four-hour ritual where ancient myth meets performance art, Balkan Erotic Epic explores the eroticism, spirituality and traditions of Abramović’s homeland through 13 visceral scenes Brought to life by a cast of over 70 performers, dancers, musicians and singers, the experience unfolds across Aviva Studios. Audiences are free to choose their own path – with pop-up encounters punctuating the action, bringing bodies together in dance, song and ritual Watch Fertility Rite , a fevered ritual where bodies writhe against the ground in a desperate call for fertility. Witness Massaging the Breast where women gesticulate over graves to awaken the earth. In Scaring the Gods , women bare themselves to the sky to banish storms. Listen Unafraid to push the boundaries of eroticism and performance art, Balkan Erotic Epic asks its audience a timeless question: What are our bodies truly for?

input.evidence_text: I Am The Fly at Refuge - Manchester’s Finest I Am The Fly at Refuge Thu 23rd Jul, 2026 - Sat 26th Sep, 2026 - The Refuge I Am The Fly at Refuge The Refuge, Oxford St, Manchester M60 7HA Thu 23rd Jul, 2026 - Sat 26th Sep, 2026 Free More info Directions events Go to Manchester's Finest Facebook page Go to Manchester's Finest X profile Original screen-printed posters and photographs from Manchester’s pioneering early 1980s music scene are being brought together for I Am The Fly, a free exhibition curated by artist Matthew Rich. Created to promote the club nights, artists and venues that helped shape the city’s cultural identity, many of the works were originally flyposted and never intended to survive. Expect rare material connected to the Haçienda era, FAC51 and the wider scene that established Manchester as a global music city. Interested in more events? We’re inviting young people to explore music for film and compose their own soundtrack this summer! Whether your child already attends our Saturday Music Club , or just wants to try something new, our Young Musician’s Summer School is suitable for all abilities! . . . . . Across this 5 day course, we’ll be exploring music for film, and working on composing music for the silent film ‘A Trip to the Moon’. The course will conclude with a live performance soundtracking the film for family and friends. . . . . We welcome all abilities and instruments to take part in this course. You can bring your own instruments, but we have keyboards, guitars, bass, violins, drums and percussion on site which can be used if you don’t have an instrument.. . . Snacks and drinks provided, and pizzas on the final day. Please bring your own packed lunch to sessions Monday-Thursday.. . Dates: Monday 17th – Friday 21st August. Times: 10:00-16:30 Monday-Thursday, then a performance for friends and family on Friday at 13:00 (students will be expected to come in at 10:00 sharp on the Friday).. Ages: 12-18 years old. Cost: £150 for the full course. Bursaries are available for those on lower income – please email learning@bandonthewall.org to arrange this.. . Spaces are limited – Book here . . . title: Mike Rice

input.lead: RNCM | Live Nation in association with UTA | 2026-09-27 | tickets
input.summary: RNCM | Live Nation in association with UTA | 2026-09-27 | tickets
input.evidence_text: There’s a cruelty to Mike Rice, a wickedness. In this show Mike talks about doing psychedelics with his brother, an entanglement with a Spanish Sausage Dog named Nino, and many other such rotten adventures ‘ Ireland has yet another legendary comedian ‘ – Dan Nightingale Irish farmer’s son, and general Nasty Character Mike Rice is one of Ireland and the UK’s fastest rising comedy stars. Having started comedy in 2016 Mike has amassed a large online following from podcast and stand-up clips, regularly getting millions of views on various platforms Mike began stand-up comedy in Chicago, where within two years he was working regularly at the most prestigious clubs in the city, including The Laugh Factory. He has since returned home because his mother made him, and has been quickly rising in the Irish and European scene, play Mike brought his first solo show The Best Boy in Ireland to the Edinburgh Fringe Festival 2018, in what was a sold-out run, and performed that same show to sold-out rooms at the Perth Fringe Festival in 2019 As well as co-hosting Mike & Vittorio’s Guide to Parenting with Vittorio Angelone, and Big Mike and The Chief with Rob Moriarty, Mike has appeared multiple times on smash hit podcast Have a Word and released his first comedy special in October 2023

title: Спектакль "Скамейка"

input.lead: Друзья, а вы давно ощущали настоящую магию театра?
input.summary: Друзья, а вы давно ощущали настоящую магию театра? 29 ноября 2026 года в Logan Hall — легендарная «Скамейка» с Алексеем Паниным
input.evidence_text: Спектакль «Скамейка» в Лондоне | Алексей Панин | 29 ноября 2026 :: EventFirst Home double_arrow Events double_arrow Спектакль "Скамейка" On sale Спектакль "Скамейка" schedule 29 Nov 2026 Sunday, 19:00 location_on Logan Hall 20 Bedford Way, London WC1H 0AL balcony 6:00 pm gpp_maybe NO REFUNDS Buy On MAP Buy Tickets Logan Hall £30.00 Select Logan Hall £35.00 Select Logan Hall £40.00 Select Logan Hall £45.00 Select Logan Hall £50.00 Select Logan Hall £55.00 Select Logan Hall £60.00 Select Logan Hall £65.00 Sold Out Logan Hall £70.00 Select Logan Hall £80.00 Select Logan Hall £85.00 Sold Out Logan Hall £90.00 Select Друзья, а вы давно ощущали настоящую магию театра? 29 ноября 2026 года в Logan Hall — легендарная «Скамейка» с Алексеем Паниным. После аншлагов в Европе и США спектакль впервые приезжает в Лондон. 📍 University College London, Level 1, 20 Bedford Way, London WC1H 0AL 🕕 DOORS: 18:00 🕖 SHOW: 19:00 «Скамейка» — знаменитая пьеса, когда-то блистательно сыгранная Олегом Ефремовым и Татьяной Дорониной на сцене МХАТа, а затем покорившая театральные площадки по всему миру. Это история о мужчине и женщине. О случайной встрече, которая постепенно превращается в откровенный разговор — честный, смешной, ранимый и очень человеческий. О том моменте, когда между людьми исчезают роли и, наконец, становится невозможно притворяться. На сцене — Алексей Панин и Ольга Силина. Тонкая актерская игра, живая энергия и удивительно узнаваемая история, в которой каждый услышит что-то своё. “Скамейка” — спектакль, после которого хочется немного помолчать и еще долго думать о главном. Если вам не хватает настоящего театра — этот вечер нельзя пропустить. Количество билетов ограничено. Продолжительность спектакля — 2 часа с антрактом. settings Loading venue plan... shopping_cart Your tickets: 0

Founded by brothers Ben and Joe Wright, Porta takes its cues from the neighbourhood bars of Spain: short menus, good produce, For the next Open Fire Dining Club , that Spanish influence is being taken out of Porta’s dining rooms and into the open air at Torr Vale Mill. From emerging technologies to regulation, customer experience to operational reality, the According to Worldpay’s Global Payments Report 2026, looking at the UK, we are a card-first society, with cash only estimated to account for 7-9% of payments by 2030 The major growing trend is the adoption of digital wallets such as Apple Pay, Google Pay and PayPal Digital wallets are the leading payment method in the UK and also globally, now making up 56% of ecommerce and 33% of POS value, respectively For businesses not just looking to scale but to even stay competitive, the offering of digital wallets and alternate payment methods is a must!

The panel b A key theme throughout the discussion was recognising the signals that it is time to move on from legacy software and to modernise tech stacks. Richard said the trigger often comes from customers rather than technology itself. At Lloyds Banking Group (LBG), a move to Google Cloud Mariam pointed to developer experience and security as critical drivers for change. Systems built on older frameworks, such as early Ruby on Rails stacks, can become difficult to maintain or secure over time. She explained, “You don’t want to be running systems with outdated pack Chris explained that with Street being a relatively new business, their challenge is different - they don’t have legacy tech but they are scaling at such a pace that growth is doubling or tripling every year. This means that early architectural decisions can be constraining, but Discussion touched on bringing stakeholders on board with any upgrades or transformation. Chris and Mariam both reinforced the need for a strong narrative, effectively ‘selling’ the journey to non-technical stakeholders and bringing people on the journey with you Richard talked about pace of delivery, and said: “If you haven’t made the right decisions, the pace of delivery will change down the line. input.evidence_text: What does the future of food production look like in the North? From lab-based breakthroughs to field level technologies, Greater Manchester is home to a growing wave of agricultural biotech and agtech pioneers redefining how we grow, manage and distribute food This event brings together 4 companies leading that transformation: Bright Biotech , Gennate , Mushroom+ , and SporeSense . input.evidence_text: Children and young people across Greater Manchester can benefit from free unlimited bus travel on the Bee Network this summer As part of the government’s Great British Summer Savings campaign, children aged 5-15 can travel for free in England, including on Bee Network bus services, throughout August In addition, 16-year-olds in Greater Manchester will be able to activate their Our Pass one month earlier (from Sat 1 Aug instead of Tue 1 Sep), giving them an extra month of free bus and cheaper tram travel Anyone aged between 16 and 18 on Mon 31 Aug 2026 and a resident of Greater Manchester is eligible to apply for a digital Our Pass via the Bee Network app, with a one-off £10 admin fee. From years spent languishing in post-industrial decline to being crowned one of ‘the coolest neighbourhoods in the world’ in the blink of an eye. What’s the story here? Find out with Jonathan Schofield Tours, with whom you’ll track Ancoats’ changes by strolling its streets, crossing its canals, and taking a look inside one of its most fascinating buildings Starting at Cutting Room Square, your expert tour guide will reveal the layers of history that make Ancoats special, from its place in establishing Manchester’s reputation as ‘Cottonopolis’ to the notorious street gangs, or ‘scuttlers’, who once patrolled and fought here But this winsome stroll is also about the urban regeneration and cultural energy of Ancoats today, which boasts everything from grassroots theatres to Manchester’s only Michelin-starred restaurant

This guided walking tour will bring those stories to life. Led by a local expert, the experience is set to be a lively introduction to the people, places and ideas that have shaped one of the UK’s most influential cities The tour will begin at Manchester Central Library, that impossible to miss, grand circular landmark on St Peter’s Square. The neoclassical design has made it one of the city’s most-loved buildings, holding thousands of stories in its walls and on its shelves. The square itself ha From here, the tour takes you through the city’s heart, down the colourful streets of the Gay Village and the calming Sackville Gardens. It is here that you will see the monuments that honour Manchester’s role in the fight for equality and the city’s pioneering LGBTQ+ community a This guided tour is filled with monuments and tales from many moons ago. The Medieval Quarter is the perfect place to soak in all that history. It is here where you will see the oldest public library in the English-speaking world, Chetham’s Library . Housed in a beautiful sandsto During all this walking, your guide will point out local street art, hidden corners and stories you’d easily miss on your own. Tales of suffragettes, reformers, musicians and everyday Mancunians who’ve all left their mark on the city in their own way

input.evidence_text: Bolton Car Boot Sale | Bolton Sunday Car Boot Sale | Times, Prices & Info Skip to content 210+ UK car boot sales — find one near you this weekend Type your postcode or town to find sales near you Search car boot sales About This Car Boot Sale Bolton Car Boot Sale is one of the North West's biggest weekly boot sales, held in the hardstanding car park of the Macron Stadium (formerly Reebok), Burnden Way, Horwich, Bolton BL6 6JW. It runs every Sunday from February right through to December, drawing over 300 sellers and thousands of bargain hunters each week. Sellers can arrive and set up from 6:00 AM, with buyers welcomed in from 7:00 AM. Entry costs just £1 per car for buyers. Pitches for sellers are £14 for a car, £20 for a van and £3 for a trailer, with regular and corner pitches available on request. Bring exact change to keep the queue moving, and call organiser Andrew on 07470784644 for more details. Expect hundreds of stalls across the concrete hardstanding pitches, so it goes ahead in most weather. On-site you'll find a 5-star catering service, doughnuts and coffee, ice cream and desserts, a meat auction, fresh fruit and veg, plants, and retro sweet stalls. The sale is dog-friendly, has male and female toilets, ample parking, and kids' rides and bouncy castles to keep little ones entertained. Last confirmed open: 8 June 2026. title: Rodri makes history after World Cup triumph

input.lead: Rodri makes history after World Cup triumph
input.summary: And he’s just the seventh male player in world football to win a domestic league title, World Cup, continental title with his country, the Champions League and the Ballon d’Or. The midfielder completed the remarkable feat after leading Spain to a 1-0 extra time win over Argentina
input.evidence_text: And he’s just the seventh male player in world football to win a domestic league title, World Cup, continental title with his country, the Champions League and the Ballon d’Or. The midfielder completed the remarkable feat after leading Spain to a 1-0 extra time win over Argentina in the 2026 World Cup final. And to underline his importance to La Roja’s achievement, Rodri also won the Golden Ball for player of the tournament. It’s the latest triumph of an astonishing career that has also seen him win 13 trophies in a City shirt. Since moving to the Etihad Stadium in July 2019, he has won four Premier League titles, two FA Cups and four League Cups. His greatest moment for the Club came in the 2023 Champions League final when he scored the winning goal in our 1-0 victory over Inter in Istanbul. As well as winning the UEFA Super Cup and FIFA Club World Cup with City the following year, Rodri then helped Spain to European Championship success – winning the Golden Ball for the tournament’s best player. That led him to becoming City’s first player to win the Ballon d’Or, a deserved accolade for an incredible year. Now adding a World Cup winner’s medal to his collection, the 30-year-old becomes the only footballer to ever the four biggest trophies for England-based players and the highest individual award available. Ten other players have won the World Cup, Champions League and Ballon d’Or with Rodri become the 11th and first Spaniard to do so. Germany legends Franz Beckenbauer and Gerd Muller and France great Zinedine Zidane have also won a domestic league title and the European Championships. Brazil’s Rivaldo and Ronaldinho and Argentina’s Lionel Messi have also won a domestic league title and the Copa America – the South American championship. Everyone at City would like to

title: Argentina suffer from latest FIFA update as Lisandro Martinez gets new setback

input.lead: Manchester United defender Lisandro Martinez was part of the Argentina team that caused global embarrassment at the World Cup 2026 final
input.summary: Manchester United defender Lisandro Martinez was part of the Argentina team that caused global embarrassment at the World Cup 2026 final
input.evidence_text: Football Manchester United FC Argentina suffer from latest FIFA update as Lisandro Martinez gets new setback Manchester United defender Lisandro Martinez was part of the Argentina team that caused global embarrassment at the World Cup 2026 final Comments Sport Ben Crawford Lisandro Martinez could be set for further disappointment as the effects of Argentina's embarrassing display at the World Cup final continue to have an impact. And it could lead to further punishment for the Manchester United star's team ahead of his return to club duties Argentina were dethroned as World Cup champions on Sunday following a 1-0 extra-time defeat to Spain in New Jersey. The centre-back was a first-half casualty after going down with injury , except that's unlikely to be the end of his dismay Martinez, 28, and his side hoped to become just the third nation in history to win back-to-back World Cups. It wasn't meant to be as their regressive tactics were widely panned, while Argentina's post-match attacks on Spain drew even more vigorous criticism The aftermath severely soured the occasion, with the Argentinian Football Federation now expected to face substantial sanctions. Disgraceful post-match incidents unfolded on Sunday as Leandro Paredes shoved Eric Garcia's throat and grappled Gavi to the turf, while substituted Mar 'The Butcher' deserves some credit for his efforts intervening in the mess. Martinez, who has already endured long injury stints during his time at Old Trafford, now faces an uncertain wait to discover the extent of his latest setback just as United's pre-season gets underway

title: The Premier League has a new transfer benchmark but Manchester United are ignoring it

input.lead: Man Utd haven't broken their transfer record since 2016 and they don't look likely to do so again this summer
input.summary: Man Utd haven't broken their transfer record since 2016 and they don't look likely to do so again this summer
input.evidence_text: Football Manchester United Transfer News The Premier League has a new transfer benchmark but Manchester United are ignoring it Man Utd haven't broken their transfer record since 2016 and they don't look likely to do so again this summer. Comments Sport opinion Tyrone Marsha There was a time earlier this year when it looked like Manchester United might have to break their transfer record this summer, with sizeable price tags slapped on midfielders such as Elliot Anderson and Adam Wharton But with six weeks of the window to go, it already seems clear that Paul Pogba's status as United's most expensive ever signing will remain untouched. There can't be many Premier League clubs whose record signing hasn't been broken in a decade United have cooled on the idea of signing Wharton, baulked at a £116million price tag for Elliot Anderson, and have shown no interest in returning to Carlos Baleba after Brighton demanded £100million last summer Get the best United news, updates and analysis straight to your Inbox courtesy of the Manchester Evening News! Mateus Fernandes was too expensive at £85million and again United have tried to be sensible this transfer window. Chief executive Omar Berrada touched on that approach at the end of last season

input.evidence_text: Manchester suggested as UK ‘pilot’ city for rent caps and controls to address issues of 'stark inequality and affordability' Government asked to devolve powers on rents to new GM mayor Economic Development Declan Carey and Local Democracy Reporter Estate agents line the streets in one of Manchester's most affluent suburbs Walking down the ever-busy Wilmslow Road in Didsbury, it's difficult to go far without seeing adverts for plush apartments or investment opportunities Prices in this part of south Manchester are at a premium, with some signs listed in shop windows offering places to rent for up to £1,500 a month You expect to pay more to live in Didsbury than other parts of the city.
